item.title: Working on walking on Tuesday.
item.description: My walking continues to improve. I was able to change surfaces without hesitating. I was able to turn the corners a lot easier. My speed was really good and I was able to keep my balance. I hope everyone is having a wonderful weekend.

item.dc:creator: wolfwinter

item.title: Tired
item.description: Story time (it’s a long six years) - end of 2018-19: I’m in a 6 year domestic partnership with a woman. We are in the process of buying a house. I’ve been open since a teen and with all my partners about being pansexual, but I come out as trans. My partner says she accepts me but is f** bashing me behind my back to her friends. She cheats on me and tells me she doesn’t want to be with a crossdressing f**. Covid hits and she leaves with the car and savings. I’m unemployed and homeless, my poor cat is left in my abandoned family home while I figure out how to not die. 2020-21: my family members and I aren’t talking, I’m living in my Jeep and working construction again to eat. This feels like an eternity. 2022: I have found a decent government job, a new apartment, and I meet someone who accepts me. Well, accepts me for my gender but not for what I do and how much I make. They work in medicine and “expect their partner to make at least 6 figures.” I battle through this because you know, being accepted as trans is a powerful thing. 2023: I’m slowly getting promotions, struggling to pay bills. I keep on it. My partner and I keep fighting and they call me the worst names. Consistently. I do my best to read about communication and try and work with them. Start to realize that they are manipulative. End of 2023-24: I finally get a good position and I’m making the most money I’ve ever made. I have a nice car and a nice apartment, things are settling out with my partner, talking about the serious life choices, like buying a house etc. Then bam, car accident and injuries. A couple weeks later my cat dies. A couple months later my brother dies tragically. My partner says they don’t have space for me and can’t be there for me. We don’t talk. They come back and tell me they found god and start to explain why I should believe and how much everything makes sense. I can’t take it. I’ve been good about letting people believe what they want but I just can’t take that talk while grieving… my brother who I hadn’t spoken to in two years was also born again and had told me in the past if I was ever a f****t he’d kill me. My brothers are the only people who I’m not out too because they are intense. Things were getting better though and I had planned to tell them. Now I’m filled with guilt because he will never know and I want to reach out to my other brother about it but I feel guilty and don’t want to add to the grief and make it seem about me. So I’m dealing with all that and my partner calls me a demon, says I’m darkness, that I steal their light and moves. Present: I’m just here fucking mad and confused and tired and honestly don’t think there’s anything really good anymore. I’m so tired of being alone, I grew up poor and a punk and never had anything and now I’m old and don’t have much of anything. None of it has seemed worth it. I’ve completely regressed into my shell of masculinity and hiding from my family. And yeah, thanks for reading I just feel invisible Edit - thanks for all the kind words. Imgur is a great community and I appreciate it
item.dc:creator: nibblesandbytes

item.title: There's always tomorrow....?
item.description: Me and my wife were together for over 20 years. She died in my arms 3 years ago, and I went the way of the downward spiral of alchohol. I've pulled myself together with the help of amazing friends and family. But it's getting harder and harder every day, not being able to hold someone. Thx for going with me on this pitty trip, I live you all, Imgur!!!
